title: T-041 — PWA scaffold (Workbox manifest + service worker registration in apps/web)
prompt:
# T-041 — PWA scaffold (Workbox manifest + service worker registration in apps/web)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 frontend (PWA Workbox dla 06-SCANNER-P1)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Deliver the PWA scaffold for apps/web: Workbox-based service worker, web app manifest, and Next.js wiring so that 06-SCANNER-P1 (downstream) can rely on installable PWA + offline shell. PRD §5 says explicitly: 'PWA (Workbox) dla 06-SCANNER-P1: Service Worker + IndexedDB sync queue + DataWedge keyboard-wedge'.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add @serwist/next (maintained Workbox-on-Next.js wrapper) to apps/web dependencies. (2) Create apps/web/app/manifest.ts exporting the Web App Manifest (name='Monopilot', short_name='Monopilot', start_url='/', display='standalone', icons 192/512). (3) Create apps/web/app/sw.ts as the Workbox service-worker entry: precache the app shell + runtime CacheFirst for static + NetworkFirst for /api with 5s timeout. (4) Wire next.config.mjs to compile the SW via withSerwist. (5) Add a register-sw client component apps/web/app/_components/RegisterSW.tsx mounted in root layout that calls navigator.serviceWorker.register on production builds only.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/app/manifest.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/sw.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/_components/RegisterSW.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/layout.tsx [modify]
- apps/web/next.config.mjs [modify]
- apps/web/package.json [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a production build runs, when 'pnpm --filter web build' completes, then a /sw.js bundle is emitted alongside /manifest.webmanifest and the manifest serves name='Monopilot' + start_url='/' + display='standalone'
2. Given the production app is running, when a browser loads /, then RegisterSW calls navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js') exactly once and the SW reaches activated state
3. Given a NetworkFirst route /api/health, when the network is offline (mocked in test), then the SW falls back to cached response within the 5-second timeout instead of throwing
4. Given a development build runs, when 'pnpm --filter web dev' starts, then RegisterSW does NOT register the SW (dev safety — no stale precache)

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/web/app/_components/__tests__/RegisterSW.test.tsx asserting prod registers / dev does not + a Vitest unit on the manifest export covering AC1 + AC4 before the components exist
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not enable PWA in dev — stale precache breaks Next.js HMR
- Do not implement IndexedDB sync queue here (covered by T-043/T-044)
- Do not register Capacitor wrapper — that is the §5 P2 fallback, not Foundation scope

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-001

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Workbox-based service worker, web app manifest, and dev-safe SW registration for apps/web — substrate for 06-SCANNER-P1 PWA.
pipeline_inputs.details: Foundation owns the PWA primitive; downstream modules (06-SCANNER) consume it. We use @serwist/next (Workbox successor on Next.js App Router) because it ships first-class App Router support and avoids the abandoned next-pwa.
out_of_scope: Do not modify files outside the scope_files list
Do not introduce business logic from later module PRDs (01-NPD, 02-SETTINGS, etc.)
